prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_svr', seasonality_champion='NaiveSeasonal', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.15) and high seasonality variance (0.80), with models capturing seasonal patterns very differently. The true series consistently trends downward across all folds, and while ONLY_CWT_catboost dominates overall RMSE, early/late horizon specialists differ slightly. These conditions strongly support DBA combination with mixed weighting to leverage diverse model strengths.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_FT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_CWT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "late": "FT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_CWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "FT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "rf", "ETS", "THETA"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "DWT_svr", "DWT_catboost", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.1512,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.15) and high seasonality variance (0.80), with models capturing seasonal patterns very differently. The true series consistently trends downward across all folds, and while ONLY_CWT_catboost dominates overall RMSE, early/late horizon specialists differ slightly. These conditions strongly support DBA combination with mixed weighting to leverage diverse model strengths."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=5 indicates horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=3.69 (high disagreement) supports dba_combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. RMSE spread ratio=1.15 and high_seasonality_variance=0.80 justify mixed weighting approaches. topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 adds diversity with k=5 matching recommended_knobs.top_k. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides shrinkage-based balancing."
}
raw.statistician:
{
  "add_names": ["best_single_by_validation"],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "High disagreement (relative_spread_mean=3.69) strongly favors weighted/selection methods which are already well-represented. best_single_by_validation appears in top of both RMSE (652.05) and MAPE (2.54) leaderboards, outperforming current candidates. n_windows=3 is low, so keeping current shrinkage settings as recommended. Current set has good diversity (dba, inverse_rmse, best_per_horizon, topk_mean). Adding best_single_by_validation provides additional selection method coverage without overfitting risk.",
  "changes": ["added best_single_by_validation for additional selection method diversity"],
  "when_good": "When n_windows increases to ≥6, consider adding ridge_stacking methods. When disagreement decreases below 0.25, baseline_mean could be reconsidered."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.15) and high seasonality variance (0.80), with models capturing seasonal patterns very differently. The true series consistently trends downward across all folds, and while ONLY_CWT_catboost dominates overall RMSE, early/late horizon specialists differ slightly. These conditions strongly support DBA combination with mixed weighting to leverage diverse model strengths.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=5 indicates horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=3.69 (high disagreement) supports dba_combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. RMSE spread ratio=1.15 and high_seasonality_variance=0.80 justify mixed weighting approaches. topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 adds diversity with k=5 matching recommended_knobs.top_k. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides shrinkage-based balancing.

statistician.rationale: High disagreement (relative_spread_mean=3.69) strongly favors weighted/selection methods which are already well-represented. best_single_by_validation appears in top of both RMSE (652.05) and MAPE (2.54) leaderboards, outperforming current candidates. n_windows=3 is low, so keeping current shrinkage settings as recommended. Current set has good diversity (dba, inverse_rmse, best_per_horizon, topk_mean). Adding best_single_by_validation provides additional selection method coverage without overfitting risk.
changes: added best_single_by_validation for additional selection method diversity
statistician.when_good: When n_windows increases to ≥6, consider adding ridge_stacking methods. When disagreement decreases below 0.25, baseline_mean could be reconsidered.
